chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ يُتْرَكُ مِنَ الْوَرَثَةِ بَعْضُهُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ وَ بَعْضُهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 39 – The one who leaves the inheritors, some of them being Muslims and some of them being Polytheists

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/219)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/219)
text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلًا ذِمِّيّاً أَسْلَمَ وَ أَبُوهُ حَيٌّ وَ لِأَبِيهِ وَلَدٌ غَيْرُهُ ثُمَّ مَاتَ الْأَبُ وَرِثَهُ الْمُسْلِمُ جَمِيعَ مَالِهِ وَ لَمْ يَرِثْهُ وَلَدُهُ وَ لَا امْرَأَتُهُ مَعَ الْمُسْلِمِ شَيْئاً .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hassan Al Taymi, from his brother Ahmad Bin Al Hassan, from his father, from Ja’far Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Rabat, raising it, said,

‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If a Zimmy man (Jew or a Christian under the protection) becomes a Muslim while his father is alive, and for his father are children other than him, then the father dies, the Muslim would inherit the entirety of his wealth, and his (other) children and his wife would not inherit anything along with the Muslim’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ali ibn al-Hassan al-Tamimiy from his brother Ahmad ibn al-Hassan from his father from Ja‘far ibn Muhammad from ibn Ribat in a marfu’ manner has said, the following: “’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ’If a Dhimmiy (taxpayer) becomes a Muslim when his father is living who has children besides him, then the father dies, the Muslim inherits all of his legacy and his other children and wife do not inherit anything with a Muslim.’”
